text: Literally, ‘The plea of the poor man will come from the mouth as far as his ears’. Some interpret this as meaning the ears of divine justice, but there is no hint of this in the text.
In the Latin version, ‘wisdom’, in the Greek text, ‘knavery’; cf. note on 19.20.

vv. 25-27: These verses are usually understood as an instruction in the usages of polite society. It is more probable that the sacred author is denouncing, under a metaphor, the habit of rash enquiry.
‘The foul fiend’ may, in the Hebrew text, have meant simply ‘his enemy’.
